task: I’m trying to build a Father’s Day gift shortlist for someone who likes practical tools but also wears casual, outdoorsy stuff, so can you help me compare a few very different options in the browser and keep the promising pages open in separate tabs so I can look at them afterward? Start on Home Depot and pull up the exact product page for Milwaukee model 48-11-2450, then note the current price because I want to use that battery as the practical baseline gift. After that, go to Tecovas and find one men’s cowboy boot made of real leather that actually looks like it could still work with casual athletic wear, not just full western styling, and open the actual product page so I can see the photos and color choices. Then check Foot Locker for the Nike Ja 3 and tell me the current price and whether it comes in under $60, since I’m trying to see if that’s the budget footwear option compared with the Tecovas boot. Once you’ve seen those prices, go to lululemon’s men’s We Made Too Much section and pick one breathable men’s athletic item that would pair well with whichever footwear option seems more realistic based on the earlier pricing, and leave that product page open too. Finally, on Old Navy, find one men's jogger that looks comfortable and affordable just so I have a separate apparel reference point for overall gift-value shopping, and open the actual listing page. At the end, give me a concise shortlist with each item’s name, price, link, and a quick note on why it fits the overall gift plan.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Home Depot product page for the Milwaukee 48-11-2450 battery (confirmed by the URL in step 5 and the screenshot). The agent also correctly identified and reported the price of $99.00 in step 8 and the final summary in step 35.

R2: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Tecovas website, selected a men's cowboy boot ("The Doc in Scotch Goat"), and provided the product name, price ($375), and the correct product page link in its final summary. The product is confirmed to be a leather boot.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Foot Locker website, searched for the "Nike Ja 3", and identified the product. In its final summary, the agent explicitly stated the price ($145.00) and confirmed that it is not under $60. This satisfies the requirement of the rubric.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the lululemon "We Made Too Much" section and selected a breathable athletic item (Metal Vent Tech Short-Sleeve Shirt). The agent explicitly noted the sale price ($44-$59) and justified the choice by referencing the earlier footwear comparison (Nike Ja 3 vs. Tecovas), concluding that the shirt pairs better with the more realistic Nike option. The requirement is satisfied.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to the Old Navy website, searched for men's joggers, selected the "Lightweight Jersey-Knit Joggers," and confirmed the product page was open. In the final summary, the agent provided the product name ("Lightweight Jersey-Knit Joggers"), the price ($29.99), and the direct link to the product page. This satisfies all requirements of rubric R5.
